contracts.new_symbol: GraphNeighbors(g *CSRGraph, fileID uint64, depth int, edgeTypeFilter EdgeType, maxNodes int) ([]CSREdge, error)
contracts.consumers_today: none in-repo yet (query-agent, agents/query/, is a future milestone per docs/LLD/graph.md's 'Interactions with other modules' section; not implemented yet)

risk_rationale: Purely additive new read-only file; does not modify csr.go, edge.go, edgelog.go, or compact.go. No concurrency surface of its own (CSRGraph is immutable once built/loaded, per csr.go's own doc comment) so no new lock-ordering seam is introduced, unlike 3.1.2/3.1.3's edge-log/compaction interaction which produced this package's two prior severe bugs. Primary risk is purely in traversal-logic correctness (BFS dedup, cap truncation, ordering) rather than durability/crash-safety, since this subtask never writes to disk.
out_of_scope: No change to csr.go, edge.go, edgelog.go, compact.go, edge_append.go.
No merge of not-yet-compacted EdgeLog entries into traversal results (see requirement.md's resolved design question) - deliberately deferred/rejected, not a gap.

No wiring into agents/query/ (query-agent) - that consumer does not exist yet in this repo per docs/LLD/graph.md; out of scope for this subtask's Impacted modules list (engine/graph/traverse.go, engine/graph/traverse_test.go only).
